name: Til Laddu (Sesame Balls)
tagline: Sesame and jaggery winter laddus — calcium, copper, magnesium
highlight: Sesame at 975mg calcium per 100g — most calcium-dense seed in your kitchen

hero.alt: Round dark-brown sesame laddus stacked on a brass plate
hero.caption: til laddu — sesame and jaggery balls

healthFacts: Sesame seeds deliver calcium, copper, magnesium, and zinc together — rare bone-health combo.
Lignans (sesamin, sesamolin) support cholesterol metabolism and antioxidant pathways.
Vitamin E in sesame oil supports skin barrier and reduces oxidative stress.
Traditional winter food — warming and energy-dense for cold months.

steps.title: Roast the sesame
steps.detail: Dry-roast sesame seeds on low heat in a wide pan for 4–5 minutes, stirring constantly, until they pop slightly and turn golden. Set aside.
steps.tip: Sesame burns quickly — keep heat low and never stop stirring.

steps.title: Melt the jaggery
steps.detail: Heat ghee in the same pan. Add jaggery, stir on low till fully melted and smooth — about 3 minutes. Test with the cold water hard-crack check.
steps.tip: Don't let it boil hard — sesame jaggery is delicate and burns fast.

steps.title: Combine and shape
steps.detail: Stir in roasted sesame, cardamom, and crushed peanuts. Mix off heat for 20 seconds. While still warm enough to handle, grease your palms with ghee and roll into walnut-sized balls. Cool fully before storing.
steps.tip: If the mixture cools too much, return to low heat for a few seconds — but don't overheat.
